questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red, so prepare to stop
Stop and wait for a green arrow
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red, so you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a flashing red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Treat it the same as a stop sign
Stop only if other vehicles are present
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means come to a complete stop and proceed when it is safe, just like at a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means slow down and proceed carefully while watching for cross traffic.

questions.prompt: What does a solid white line on the road indicate?
choices: You may pass freely across it
It marks the edge of the road or discourages lane changes
It separates traffic moving in opposite directions
questions.explanation: A solid white line marks the road edge or discourages crossing between lanes moving in the same direction.

questions.prompt: An eight-sided (octagonal) sign always means what?
questions.explanation: A red octagon is always a stop sign and means you must come to a complete stop.

questions.prompt: What color and shape are most warning signs that alert you to hazards ahead?
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn drivers of hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: What does an orange traffic sign indicate?
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and work zones where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow center line on the road means what?
choices: You may pass when it is safe to do so
Two-way traffic merges into one lane
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line means you may cross to pass when the way ahead is clear and it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal at an intersection mean?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow
Stop and wait for a steady green
The arrow is only a warning
questions.explanation: A green arrow means you may proceed in the direction it points, as that movement is protected.

questions.prompt: What should you do when approaching an intersection where the traffic signals are completely dark and not working?
choices: Treat it as a four-way stop
Stop only if you see police
questions.explanation: When signals are out, treat the intersection as an all-way stop and yield appropriately.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at exactly the same time. Who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an all-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are making a left turn at an intersection, who must you yield to?
choices: No one, since you are turning
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: In Utah, when may you turn right on a red light?
choices: After a complete stop, if no sign prohibits it and the way is clear
Only if no traffic light camera is present
questions.explanation: You may turn right on red after stopping completely and yielding, unless a sign prohibits the turn.

questions.prompt: Who always has the right-of-way in a marked crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians who are crossing within a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: When you approach a roundabout, who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: You must yield to traffic already circulating in the roundabout before you enter.

questions.prompt: What should you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
questions.explanation: Yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the road and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: When entering a highway from an on-ramp, what is your responsibility?
choices: Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
Match traffic speed and merge when there is a safe gap
Force your way into the nearest lane
Drive on the shoulder until traffic clears
questions.explanation: Use the on-ramp to accelerate to traffic speed and merge smoothly into a safe gap.

questions.prompt: Where should you position your vehicle before making a left turn from a two-way street?
choices: Close to the center line in the left portion of your lane
questions.explanation: Position near the center line before a left turn so you can complete it into the proper lane.

questions.prompt: A vehicle is already stopped at an uncontrolled T-intersection on the through road. If you are on the road that ends, what should you do?
choices: Yield to traffic on the through road
Assume traffic will stop for you
questions.explanation: A driver on the terminating road of a T-intersection must yield to traffic on the continuing through road.

questions.prompt: When should you begin signaling before making a turn?
choices: Well before the turn so others can react
Only if other cars are nearby
questions.explanation: Signal early so other drivers and pedestrians know your intentions before you turn.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in a Utah urban or residential district?
questions.explanation: Utah's default speed limit in urban and residential districts is 25 mph unless a sign says otherwise.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit posted on some Utah interstate highways?
questions.explanation: Some Utah interstate highways are posted as high as 80 mph where clearly marked.

questions.prompt: What does the 'basic speed law' require of every driver?
choices: Always drive the posted limit exactly
Drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for conditions
Match the speed of the car ahead
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires driving at a speed that is safe for traffic, weather, and road conditions.

questions.prompt: Under good conditions, what following distance does the three-second rule recommend?
choices: One second behind the vehicle ahead
At least three seconds behind the vehicle ahead
Five car lengths regardless of speed
Ten seconds behind the vehicle ahead
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives you enough space and time to react to the vehicle ahead in good conditions.

questions.prompt: How should you adjust your following distance in rain, snow, or fog?
choices: Increase it to four or more seconds
questions.explanation: Poor conditions lengthen stopping distance, so increase your following distance to four seconds or more.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your vehicle's stopping distance?
choices: It only affects braking on hills
questions.explanation: The faster you go, the more distance your vehicle needs to come to a complete stop.

questions.prompt: When should you slow down before a sharp curve?
choices: Only if the road is wet
questions.explanation: Reduce speed before entering a curve rather than braking hard inside it, where traction is reduced.

questions.prompt: What should you do if a driver behind you is following too closely (tailgating)?
choices: Speed up well over the limit
Increase your following distance and let them pass
questions.explanation: Give yourself more space ahead and allow a tailgater to pass so you are not caught between two vehicles.

questions.prompt: Why should you reduce speed when visibility is limited?
choices: Because you need more time and distance to react to hazards
Because it is required only at night
questions.explanation: Limited visibility shortens how far ahead you can see, so slowing down gives you time to react safely.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it can do what?
choices: Disrupt traffic and increase the risk of collisions
questions.explanation: Driving too slowly can block the flow of traffic and lead other drivers into unsafe passing maneuvers.

questions.prompt: When is it illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a straight road with good visibility
When a solid yellow line is on your side of the road
Whenever you are in a hurry
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because it is unsafe there.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, which lane should slower traffic use?
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right and use the left lane mainly for passing.

questions.prompt: When is it safe to return to your lane after passing a vehicle?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Returning when you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have left enough space.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what must you always do?
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and check your blind spot
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors and blind spots ensures no vehicle is in the lane you are entering.

questions.prompt: Where is passing generally prohibited?
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far ahead
questions.explanation: Passing near hills and curves is dangerous because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: Is it legal to pass another vehicle by driving on the shoulder of the road?
choices: No, passing on the shoulder is not allowed
questions.explanation: The shoulder is not a travel lane, and passing there is illegal and dangerous.

questions.prompt: What should you do when another driver is trying to pass you?
choices: Speed up to prevent the pass
Maintain or slightly reduce your speed and stay right
questions.explanation: Hold or slightly reduce your speed and keep right so the other driver can complete the pass safely.

questions.prompt: What does a center left-turn lane (marked with two-way arrows) allow you to do?
choices: Make left turns from either direction of travel
questions.explanation: A shared center turn lane is used only for making left turns from either direction, not for through travel or passing.

questions.prompt: When you must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights on an undivided road, when may you proceed?
choices: When the red lights stop flashing and the stop arm is withdrawn
When children appear to be off the road
questions.explanation: Remain stopped until the bus's red lights stop flashing and the stop arm folds back in.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of checking your blind spot before changing lanes?
choices: To see vehicles that mirrors do not show
questions.explanation: Blind spots hide vehicles your mirrors cannot reveal, so a head check prevents side collisions.

questions.prompt: In Utah, how much space must you give a bicyclist when passing?
questions.explanation: Utah law requires drivers to leave at least three feet of space when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to overlook, so check carefully before changing lanes or turning.

questions.prompt: What does Utah's Move Over law require when you approach a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Slow down and move over a lane when possible
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires you to slow down and change lanes away from stopped emergency and service vehicles when it is safe.

questions.prompt: Where are the large blind spots ('no-zones') around a big truck?
choices: Around the front, back, and both sides
questions.explanation: Trucks have wide blind spots on all sides, so avoid lingering where the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: When a large truck makes a right turn, what should you avoid doing?
choices: Squeezing between the truck and the curb on its right
Waiting for it to complete the turn
questions.explanation: Trucks swing wide to turn right, so never try to pass on the right or you may be caught in the turn.

questions.prompt: What should you do when you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog?
choices: Yield and give them extra time and space to cross
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog indicates a pedestrian who is blind, and drivers must yield the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, what following distance is recommended?
choices: A larger following distance than for a car
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly and are affected by road hazards, so allow extra following distance.

questions.prompt: What should you watch for when driving near schools and playgrounds?
choices: Children who may enter the road unexpectedly
questions.explanation: Children may dart into the street near schools and playgrounds, so slow down and stay alert.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a truck driver's mirrors, what is true?
choices: The driver can still see you clearly
You are in a safe position
questions.explanation: If you can't see the truck's side mirrors, you are in a blind spot and the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: How should you treat a bicyclist riding on the road?
choices: As an obstacle to honk at
As a legal road user with the right to share the lane
As someone who must always ride on the sidewalk
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the legal right to use the road, so share the lane and pass them safely.

questions.prompt: In Utah, what is the blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit at which a driver age 21 or older is considered legally impaired?
questions.explanation: Utah sets the legal BAC limit at 0.05 percent for drivers 21 and older, the strictest in the nation.

questions.prompt: Under Utah's 'not-a-drop' law, how much alcohol may a driver under age 21 have in their system?
choices: Any measurable amount is a violation
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 face zero tolerance, so any measurable alcohol while driving is a violation.

questions.prompt: Which of the following does Utah's distracted driving law prohibit while driving?
choices: Writing, sending, or reading a text message on a handheld device
questions.explanation: Utah bans manually writing, sending, or reading text and similar handheld device use while driving.

questions.prompt: How does alcohol affect a driver?
choices: It slows reaction time and impairs judgment
It has no effect in small amounts
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reaction time and impairs judgment and coordination, making driving unsafe.

questions.prompt: Besides illegal drugs, what else can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medications that cause drowsiness
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or impairment, so it is illegal to drive while affected by them.

questions.prompt: Utah's texting-while-driving law is a primary offense. What does that mean?
choices: You can only be cited if stopped for another reason
An officer can stop and cite you for that violation alone
questions.explanation: As a primary offense, an officer can stop and ticket you solely for illegal handheld device use.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle a phone call you must make while driving?
choices: Type quickly at a red light
Pull over to a safe location before using the phone
Hold the phone in one hand
Wait until you are speeding up
questions.explanation: Pulling over to a safe spot lets you use the phone without taking attention from driving.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to avoid driving under the influence of alcohol?
choices: Plan ahead for a sober driver or another ride
questions.explanation: Only time removes alcohol from the body, so arrange a sober driver or alternate ride in advance.

questions.prompt: What is the BAC limit for a commercial vehicle driver in Utah?
questions.explanation: Commercial drivers in Utah are held to a stricter 0.04 percent BAC limit.

questions.prompt: Drowsy driving is dangerous because it does what?
choices: Slows reaction time and can cause you to fall asleep at the wheel
questions.explanation: Fatigue slows reactions and judgment and can lead to falling asleep, much like impaired driving.

questions.prompt: In Utah, you must use your headlights whenever your windshield wipers are in continuous use. Why does this matter?
choices: It makes your vehicle more visible in rain and snow
questions.explanation: Utah requires headlights when wipers are running so your vehicle is visible in poor weather.

questions.prompt: In fog or heavy snow, which headlights should you use?
questions.explanation: Low beams reduce glare in fog and snow, while high beams reflect off moisture and worsen visibility.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, what should you do?
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Turn the wheel sharply the opposite way
questions.explanation: Ease off the accelerator and steer where you want the front of the car to go to recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: Why do bridges and overpasses freeze before other road surfaces?
choices: They are made of metal that holds heat
Cold air circulates above and below them, freezing them first
They are closer to the sun
questions.explanation: Air flows above and below bridges, so they lose heat and ice over before regular roadways.

questions.prompt: When driving on wet or icy roads, how should you brake?
choices: Pump the brakes rapidly on every stop
questions.explanation: Gentle, smooth braking helps maintain traction and avoids skids on slippery roads.

questions.prompt: What should you do if heavy fog or a storm makes it too dangerous to keep driving?
choices: Stop in your lane and wait
Pull completely off the road and turn on hazard lights
Speed up to get through it
questions.explanation: If you cannot safely continue, pull fully off the road and use hazard lights so others can see you.

questions.prompt: What is hydroplaning?
choices: When tires grip the road better in rain
When tires ride on a layer of water and lose contact with the road
When brakes lock on dry pavement
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning happens when water lifts the tires off the road, so slow down in heavy rain to prevent it.

questions.prompt: When driving at night, why should you dim your high beams for oncoming traffic?
choices: High beams can blind the oncoming driver
It is illegal to use any headlights at night
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams for approaching vehicles because the glare can temporarily blind the other driver.

questions.prompt: How should you handle the area in front of you when sun glare is severe?
choices: Slow down, use your visor, and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Sun glare reduces visibility, so slow down, use the visor, and leave extra space to react.

questions.prompt: How close to a fire hydrant are you prohibited from parking in Utah?
questions.explanation: You may not park within 15 feet of a fire hydrant so it stays accessible to firefighters.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: In Utah, children younger than 8 years old must ride in a child safety or booster seat unless they are at least how tall?
questions.explanation: Utah requires a child safety or booster seat for children under 8 unless they are at least 57 inches tall.

questions.prompt: Who is required to wear a seat belt in a vehicle in Utah?
questions.explanation: Utah law requires every occupant of the vehicle to be properly restrained.

questions.prompt: When parking on a hill facing uphill next to a curb, which way should you turn your front wheels?
choices: Away from the curb so a tire rests against it
questions.explanation: Facing uphill with a curb, turn wheels away from the curb so the tire catches the curb if the car rolls back.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
choices: On a crosswalk or within an intersection
questions.explanation: Parking on crosswalks or in intersections blocks traffic and pedestrians and is not allowed.

questions.prompt: When may you park in a space reserved for people with disabilities?
choices: When you are in a hurry
Only when you display a valid disability permit or plate
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces require a valid disability permit or license plate.

questions.prompt: After parking, what should you do to keep your vehicle from rolling?
choices: Set the parking brake and shift to park (or into gear)
questions.explanation: Setting the parking brake and placing the car in park or in gear keeps it from rolling away.

questions.prompt: Where is the safest place for a young child's car seat to be installed?
choices: The back seat, away from active airbags
questions.explanation: Children are safest in the back seat away from front airbags, which can injure them in a crash.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a learner permit in Utah?
questions.explanation: Utah allows applicants to apply for a learner permit beginning at age 15.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a Utah teen complete before getting a license?
choices: At least 40 hours, including 10 at night
questions.explanation: Utah teens must log at least 40 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of those at night.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Utah written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Utah written knowledge test consists of 50 questions.

questions.prompt: What score must you achieve to pass the Utah written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the written knowledge test questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: While driving with a learner permit in Utah, who must supervise you?
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old seated beside you
questions.explanation: A learner permit holder must be supervised by a licensed driver at least 21 years old in the front seat.

questions.prompt: How long must a Utah teen under 18 hold a learner permit before applying for a driver license?
questions.explanation: Teens under 18 must hold the learner permit for at least six months before getting a license.

questions.prompt: During the first six months of licensure, when may a new Utah driver under 18 NOT drive unsupervised?
questions.explanation: New Utah drivers under 18 generally may not drive unsupervised between midnight and 5 a.m.

questions.prompt: During the first six months after licensing, who may a new driver under 18 carry as passengers?
choices: Only one passenger of any kind
questions.explanation: For the first six months, new drivers under 18 may carry only immediate family members as passengers.

questions.prompt: Besides the written knowledge test, what other test must Utah license applicants pass?
questions.explanation: Applicants must also pass a separate test on identifying road signs.

questions.prompt: What must Utah teen drivers complete before they can be licensed?
questions.explanation: Utah teens must complete an approved driver education course as part of getting licensed.

questions.prompt: Under Utah's implied consent law, what happens if you refuse a chemical test when lawfully arrested for DUI?
questions.explanation: Refusing a chemical test under implied consent results in an automatic license suspension.

questions.prompt: What can happen if you accumulate too many points on your Utah driving record?
questions.explanation: Accumulating too many points from violations can lead to suspension of your driving privileges.

questions.prompt: What is a likely penalty for a first violation of Utah's 'not-a-drop' law by a driver under 21?
questions.explanation: A first not-a-drop violation can result in a six-month license suspension for a driver under 21.

questions.prompt: What may happen if you drive without the required insurance in Utah?
questions.explanation: Driving without required insurance can lead to fines and even impoundment of your vehicle.

questions.prompt: Why do fines often double in construction work zones?
choices: To protect workers and drivers in a higher-risk area
Because the speed limit is higher there
questions.explanation: Increased fines in work zones encourage safe driving to protect workers and other road users.

questions.prompt: Which of these is a primary offense in Utah, meaning you can be stopped for it alone?
choices: Having an air freshener on the mirror
questions.explanation: Texting while driving is a primary offense, so an officer can stop you for that alone.

questions.prompt: What is a consequence of driving on a suspended license?
choices: Additional fines and possible jail time
questions.explanation: Driving while suspended is a serious offense that can bring more fines, a longer suspension, or jail.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Utah can result in which of the following?
choices: Fines, possible jail, license suspension, and alcohol education
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction carries fines, possible jail, license suspension, and required alcohol education.

questions.prompt: What is the best way to protect your driving privileges over time?
choices: Obey traffic laws and maintain a clean record
questions.explanation: Following the law and keeping a clean record protects your license and keeps everyone safe.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash, what are you generally required to do?
choices: Stop, exchange information, and report injuries or major damage
Drive away if no police are present
Only stop if your car is damaged
questions.explanation: You must stop at a crash, exchange information, and report injuries or significant property damage.
